storage_constraint: Handshake is PostgreSQL-only; SQLite is forbidden in runtime, tests, fixtures, mocks, examples, fallbacks, cache, compatibility adapters, temporary harnesses, imports, exports, and product paths.

features.feature: Stable public character IDs separate from internal IDs
features.why: Operator-facing character identity needs stable labels without leaking storage keys.

features.feature: Typed character sheet parser with union and block-list fields
features.why: CKC solved real sheet editing pain with typed fields, descriptor fallbacks, score normalization, and nested block editing.

features.feature: Append-only sheet versions with selective apply/revert
features.why: Prevents data loss when models or imports update sheets.
Daily library work requires multi-select, bulk tags/fields, batch exports, trash, and restore.
CKC moved beyond one-off import into recoverable review work.
Contact sheets are useful for comparing generated or curated media and for handoff.

features.feature: OpenPose sidecars hidden from normal galleries
features.why: Sidecars are production artifacts but clutter normal viewing unless projected intentionally.
Operator needs PoseKit to operate on collections and individual photos, not only a single character page.
CKC already hardened an OpenPose-compatible pose representation useful for ComfyUI and image production.
Fine control over generated character pose was an evolved production need.
Stable identity references are directly tied to character consistency and ComfyUI workflows.
Real pose workflow needs multiple drafts open without deleting stored rigs.
CKC already closed the loop from generation output back into character/media lineage.

features.feature: Workflow spec registry and image-sourcing adapter
features.why: External generation/sourcing task output needs versioned ingestion and idempotency.
Prevents character names or sensitive sheet fields from leaking into paths or events.

features.feature: Global search with snippets and jump targets
features.why: Fast retrieval across sheets, notes, images, and moodboards is core Lens behavior.

features.feature: Tag manager, saved searches, palettes, dHash similarity
features.why: Convenience features became library-scale navigation requirements.
Prompt diaries and visual planning need more than static notes.

features.feature: Built-in model manual and command map
features.why: Lets no-context models operate the product.
CKC evolved toward multi-agent operation before Handshake's kernel reset.
Model-driven GUI verification must be quiet and reproducible.
Real media libraries drift; deletion must be reversible and explainable.

features.feature: Backup version traceability and orphan adoption
features.why: Recovery became a first-class operator need.

features.feature: Web portfolio and share pack exports
features.why: Operator needs portable handoff outputs.
CKC discovered the right boundary: not everything needs CRDT, but parallel edits need receipts and revisions.
Unfinished work is still requirement evidence: draggable calibration, missing-marker placement, 3D/live split editing, forked history.
